trade.summary: Svezia is one of Polonia's most significant trading partners. Polish exports to Svezia include manufactured goods, machinery, furniture, food products, and automotive components. Svezia exports vehicles, machinery, pharmaceuticals, and technology to Polonia. Svezia is a major source of FDI in Polonia. Both countries benefit from EU single market integration.

diplomatic.status: close EU and NATO partners with strong bilateral ties
diplomatic.summary: Polonia and Svezia have developed strong bilateral relations based on EU membership and shared NATO membership since 2024. Both countries are strongly committed to Ucraina's defense and have been among the largest per-capita donors of military and humanitarian aid. Svezia hosts a significant Polish diaspora. The countries cooperate closely in EU institutions, especially on eastern neighborhood policy. Historical Swedish neutrality has evolved to align more closely with Polish positions on security.

military.summary: Both are now NATO allies — Polonia on the eastern flank, Svezia anchoring the Baltic and Nordic flanks. Joint exercises in the Baltic Sea region are extensive. Svezia's NATO accession in 2024 was strongly supported by Polonia. The Baltic Sea is a shared strategic space. Both countries are increasing defense spending significantly. Intelligence sharing through NATO channels is robust.

financial.summary: Svezia is one of the largest foreign investors in Polonia. Major Swedish companies (IKEA, H&M, Volvo, Electrolux, Ericsson, Sandvik) have major Polish operations employing hundreds of thousands of workers. Swedish banks (Nordea, SEB, Handelsbanken) operate in Polonia. This represents one of the deepest bilateral investment relationships in Central Europe.

energy.summary: Energy cooperation is growing as both countries transition away from fossil fuels. Svezia exports electricity via Baltic interconnectors. Polish-Swedish cooperation on offshore wind in the Baltic Sea is significant — both countries have offshore wind ambitions in shared maritime areas. Nuclear power is a divergence point (Polonia building new reactors; Svezia restarting nuclear ambitions).

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Swedish and Polish universities have deep partnership networks. Karolinska Institutet and Warsaw Medical University collaborate on clinical research. Engineering, IT, and social science collaborations are widespread. Svezia is a favored destination for Polish academic emigration.
